items.correct: When a sentence or phrase runs over from one line of poetry to the next without a punctuated pause, creating flow or surprise across the line break.
items.misconception: Enjambment is when a poem has very long lines.
items.random_wrong: Enjambment is when a poem uses no punctuation at all.
items.why: Enjambment is about syntax crossing the line break, not line length or punctuation in general. Students miss that it is about the relationship between grammar and lineation.

items.q: Why must students distinguish the narrator from the author?
items.correct: The narrator is a constructed voice within the text; the author is the real person who created it. Conflating them misreads irony, unreliable narration and persona.
items.misconception: The narrator's views are always the author's views.
items.random_wrong: The narrator and author are different words for the same thing.
items.why: Students attribute everything a first-person narrator says to the author. This collapses irony — a novel like 'Lolita' cannot work if you take Humbert as Nabokov.

items.correct: A line of five iambs (unstressed-stressed syllable pairs), creating a ten-syllable line with the rhythm da-DUM da-DUM da-DUM da-DUM da-DUM.
items.misconception: Iambic pentameter is any line with ten syllables.
items.random_wrong: Iambic pentameter is when every line rhymes.
items.why: Syllable count alone doesn't make iambic pentameter — the stress pattern is essential. And iambic pentameter is usually unrhymed (blank verse) in Shakespeare's plays.

items.q: Which poem opens 'Suddenly he awoke and was running'?
items.why: Correct answer: 'Bayonet Charge'. “'Exposure'” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: What is the recurring refrain in 'Exposure'?
items.why: Correct answer: 'But nothing happens'. “'Into the valley of Death'” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many soldiers ride in Tennyson's brigade?
items.why: The real figure is 600. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
